policy.default: No third-party skill may be installed/used until security check result is PASS (or explicit exception approved).

downloadedSkills.name: qmd
downloadedSkills.summary: Repo appears clean overall and automated checker passed, but QMD supports config-driven shell execution via collection `update` commands (`bash -c`). Safe for trusted local use, but treat collection configs as code.

findings.evidence: src/cli/qmd.ts:523-547 runs yaml collection update commands via bash -c

mitigations: Only use trusted local QMD config files/collections
Do not accept unreviewed `update:` commands in qmd YAML config
Prefer using QMD for search/indexing only unless you explicitly need update hooks
downloadedSkills.recommendation: Approved for cautious use with mitigation; do not treat untrusted configs as safe data.

downloadedSkills.name: capability-evolver
downloadedSkills.summary: Not malicious-looking, but too powerful/risky for default use: self-modifying behavior, external network sync, automated code changes, rollback/destructive git operations, and dependency healing via npm.

findings.evidence: SKILL.md declares writes to workspace/src/** and describes autonomous evolution/solidification
SKILL.md and source reference git reset --hard / git clean -fd
SKILL.md uses evomap.ai A2A endpoints and optional GitHub/memory graph publishing

mitigations: Do not enable in this workspace for routine use
Only evaluate in isolated sandbox if ever tested
Do not grant secrets or write access to production repos
downloadedSkills.recommendation: Do not install/use as an active skill in Nick's workspace.

downloadedSkills.name: humanizer-2
downloadedSkills.summary: Low security risk. Mostly prompt guidance plus local analysis/humanization tooling. Main risk is quality/style overcorrection rather than system compromise.

findings.evidence: Skill encourages 'personality injection' including strategic typos/tangents in some contexts

mitigations: Use for editing, not as an always-on writing authority without judgment
Avoid strategic-typo guidance in professional/client-facing copy unless explicitly wanted
downloadedSkills.recommendation: Safe to use cautiously as an editing aid.

downloadedSkills.name: playwright-skill
downloadedSkills.summary: Browser automation is inherently high-impact, and this skill instructs writing/running custom Playwright scripts and installing dependencies. No obvious malicious payload, but it can take real actions on websites.

findings.evidence: SKILL.md includes login flows, form submission, external-site automation, screenshots, and scripted execution
Installed package contains docs only (SKILL.md/API_REFERENCE.md) and references run.js/lib helpers not present in package

mitigations: Use only for trusted targets and clearly scoped browser tasks
Require human intent for any external-site actions or form submissions
Do not assume the package is turnkey; verify missing runtime pieces before use
downloadedSkills.recommendation: Usable with caution for local/test-site automation; not approved as a blind autonomous web actor.

createdSkills.name: security-skill-checker
createdSkills.prompt: Create a skill that scans third-party skills for prompt-injection risk, destructive commands, secret exposure, exfiltration behavior, and privilege escalation; return PASS/WARN/FAIL with evidence and mitigations.
